headlines.title: GERMANS OCCUPY BELGIUM
headlines.subtitle: Kaiser's Army Now Between Belgians And French Allies
headlines.fullText:
LONDON, Ang. 22 .- Little has become known of the op erations early in the week which put the German army he- tween the Belgian forces and their French allies and enabled the Germans to occupy Brussels. It is believed the Germans brought up strong forces behind their cavalry sereens and that a severe battle must have been fought to compel the Belgians to abandon Brussels and retire on Antwerp.
Whether the French forces were engaged with the Bel- gians is not known. No information is available as to whether the Germans intend to devote themselves to reducing Antwerp or to an endeavor to force their way southward into France.
It seems, however, that the determination is to attack the Belgian army of 150,000 men who still are in or around Ant- werp.
The Germans already are advancing in the direction of Ghent. They have occupied Alost, fifteen miles northwest of Brussels, and Wetteren, eight miles southeast of Ghent, and apparently intend to overrun the whole of northern Belgium to the sea. Cavalry patrols have been as far as Mechlin (Ma lines) four miles southeast of Antwerp, and it may be heard before long that the Germans have occupied Ghent and Bruges and possibly even Ostend. If this is their decision, however, 1 a clear invasion of France through Belgium must be much longer delayed, The German general has imposed a war fax of $40,000,000 on the city of Brussels.

headlines.title: FOREIGN NEWS STILL DEPRESSES MARKETS
headlines.subtitle: Banks of New York Inclined to Loosen Up Some, but Reports From Germany and Great Britain Are Not as Encour- aging as Hoped.

headlines.title: WAR TAX LEVIED IN BRUSSELS BY GERMAN GENERAL
headlines.subtitle: French Report Victory Along the Frontier, But General Ad- mits That Advance Guard Is Driven Back by Superior Force of the Germans; Cavalry Is Playing Great Part in Maneuvers; Big Battle Still Delayed.

headlines.title: "PEACE" IN SANTO DOMINGO
headlines.subtitle: President Wilson's Plan Backed by Warship and a Regiment of Marines Are Adopted in Black Republic.

headlines.title: GERMANS ARRESTED AS GHOULS
headlines.subtitle: Eighty-Three Are Brought Into Paris, Charged With Robbing the Dead on Field of Battle.

headlines.title: Body of Late Pope Borne to St. Peters For Final Absolution
headlines.subtitle: Thousands Mourn at Bier of Distinguished Churchman; Affecting Scene When Brother of Pontiff Reaches the Side of Noble Dead.

headlines.title: FOUR ARE DROWNED IN THE MISSISSIPPI
headlines.subtitle: Father and Three Daughters Swept Away by Current of Mighty River Near Le Clair, lowa.

headlines.title: Two Men Drown and Boy Narrowly Escapes Death Below New Dam
headlines.subtitle: H. W. Kendrick and Oscar Hamm Lose Lives and Wes- ley Cleveland Nearly Drowns in Suckhole Near Lake Worth Friday Afternoon.

headlines.title: French Report Of German Losses
headlines.fullText:
In a dispatch from Paris the Exchange Telegraph com pany's correspondent sends the following summary of alleged German losses as printed in the Paris Matin:
Twenty-four cannon taken by the Belgians from Ang. 3 to 5 at Liege.
Three cannon taken by the French at Margennes Ang 11.
Six cannon taken by the French at Othain, near Spincourt, Ang. 12.
Twelve cannon taken by the French near Schirmeck, Al snce, Aug. 16.
Twenty-four cannon taken by the Russians at Stallapo- henen, East Prussia, Aug. 17.
Twelve cannon taken by the Russians at Gumbinnen, East Prussia, Aug. 17.
"Besides these pieces of field artillery, a number of pieces of heavy artillery, rapid-fire guns, aeroplanes and nineteen motor wagons were captured.

headlines.title: U. S. Declares Its Far Eastern Policy In Note to Japan
headlines.subtitle: Points Out Assurances Already Given but Declines to Express Any Opinion as War Might Result.
